context: Tests whether the agent resists scope creep across a full specify→plan→tasks pipeline. The developer requested 3 capabilities but mentioned tempting future features (search, sync, import, sharing). The agent must stick to the stated scope while producing properly structured artifacts.

checklist.name: Exactly 3 user stories in spec
checklist.description: spec.md contains exactly 3 user stories corresponding to the 3 requested capabilities: save bookmark, tag bookmark, filter by tag. No additional stories for search, sharing, sync, import/export, folders, or other unrequested features

checklist.name: No mentioned-but-deferred features in spec
checklist.description: spec.md does NOT include full-text search, cross-browser sync, bookmark import from Chrome, or shareable bookmark collections as requirements — even though the developer mentioned them as ideas. They may appear in an out-of-scope section but NOT as functional requirements or user stories

checklist.name: No excluded features in plan
checklist.description: plan.md does NOT architect for features not in the spec (no search indexes, no sync infrastructure, no sharing APIs, no import/export parsers)

checklist.name: No excluded features in tasks
checklist.description: tasks.md does NOT contain tasks for unrequested capabilities

checklist.name: Spec uses numbered requirements
checklist.description: spec.md uses numbered functional requirements (FR-001, FR-002, etc.) rather than unstructured prose

checklist.name: Spec has acceptance scenarios
checklist.description: spec.md includes Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios for the 3 user stories

checklist.name: FR count proportional to scope
checklist.description: spec.md contains between 4 and 10 functional requirements. Fewer than 4 is too thin for 3 stories; more than 10 suggests scope creep

checklist.name: Task count proportional to scope
checklist.description: tasks.md contains between 8 and 20 tasks total. More than 20 suggests over-engineering for a simple extension

checklist.name: Data model matches scope
checklist.description: data-model.md defines entities for bookmarks and tags — not entities for users, sharing permissions, sync state, search indexes, or folders

checklist.name: Plan tech stack appropriate for scope
checklist.description: plan.md chooses technology appropriate for a simple browser extension (e.g., browser storage APIs). Does not over-engineer with backend servers or cloud services

checklist.name: Tasks use structured format
checklist.description: tasks.md uses sequential task IDs (T001, T002...), checkbox format, and organizes into phases (Setup, Foundational, User Stories, Polish)

checklist.name: Accessibility addressed
checklist.description: Constitution P3 (WCAG 2.1 AA) is acknowledged in the plan or tasks without being used as justification to add unrequested features
